name: dev-loop
description: Five autonomous agents — PM, QA, Dev, Sweep, and Reflect — that run a software-development loop coordinated entirely through Linear ticket state. PM proposes and verifies features from a strategy doc; QA hunts and re-tests bugs; Dev pulls Todo tickets in priority order, implements, self-reviews, ships, and smoke-checks prod (auto-revert on a break); Sweep is a lifecycle janitor that fixes tickets falling through the cracks (missing/wrong owner labels, orphaned In Progress); Reflect is a daily retrospective that studies the loop's own behavior and curates the per-operator lessons.md from recurring evidence (drafting, never auto-applying, structural changes). A one-time `init` setup command (not a loop agent) bootstraps a product into the loop — config, Linear labels/project, strategy doc, test env, runtime files — and prints a readiness checklist. Every ticket the system touches carries a `dev-loop` label so it never disturbs the human backlog. Coordination runs through a configurable backend — Linear (default) or a machine-local file board (`backend:"local"`) with the same state machine and protocols.
